prompt:
Someone on the dairy-floor tooling rotation tidied our telemetry header last sprint and now nothing that includes it builds. g++ in C++20 mode says, among a great deal else:

    vatstats.h:18:5: error: need 'typename' before 'C::const_iterator' because 'C' is a dependent scope
    vatstats.h:58:47: error: template argument 2 is invalid
    vatstats.h:58:47: error: template argument 4 is invalid
    vatstats.h:42:9: error: 'readings_' was not declared in this scope

Line 58 is a perfectly ordinary map declaration with two template arguments, so I would love to know what 'argument 4' even refers to, and after that the compiler pretends half the class doesn't exist.

The summaries themselves are settled with QA -- warmest reading, plain mean, per-vat grouping, zeros for empty batches and unknown vats -- and none of that behavior may change. Also please don't "fix" the build by de-templating the helpers; the loggers hand us different container types and the genericity is load-bearing (the tests exercise it). Make 'make test' build and pass with the smallest edits; Makefile, mintest.h, and test_vat.cpp stay untouched.
